context: Rarely of clinical significance Country:   endemic to Southeast Asia: Japan, Korea, Central + South China, Taiwan, Indochina; Eastern Europe Organism:   Chinese liver fluke = Clonorchis sinensis Closely related:   Opisthorchis viverrini (Thailand and Laos); Opisthorchis felineus (Siberia) Cycle:   parasite cysts digested by gastric juice → larvae migrate up the bile ducts → remain in small intrahepatic ducts until maturity (10-30 mm in length) → travel to larger ducts to deposit eggs Infection:   snail + freshwater fish serve as intermediate hosts; infection occurs by eating raw fish; hog, dog
